# Agent Machines

> A persistent machine for your agent. One stateful microVM per Clerk account; chat history, files, learned skills, and cron schedules all live on /home/machine and survive every sleep cycle.

## Summary

Agent Machines provisions a per-account Linux microVM where the agent stores everything to disk under `/home/machine/.agent-machines/`: chat `.jsonl` files, USER.md, MEMORY.md, an FTS5 sessions database, cron schedules, the Python venv, and learned skills. The same machine wakes on every device the user signs in from with Clerk.

The product is open source (MIT) and lives at <https://github.com/Kevin-Liu-01/agent-machines>.

## Core facts

- **Persistence model**: filesystem on disk, not vendor-controlled black-box memory.
- **Boot times**: ~30s cold, <5s warm; second-billed Dedalus microVM hibernation.
- **Agents supported**: Hermes (Nous Research, memory + cron + MCP-native) and OpenClaw (Dedalus Labs reference, Anthropic computer-use loop with browser + screenshot + click). Both expose `/v1/chat/completions`.
- **Providers supported**: Dedalus Machines is live today. Vercel Sandbox and Fly Machines are present in the `MachineProvider` contract and setup UI, but their provisioners currently return not-supported.
- **Auth + fleet identity**: Clerk private metadata stores per-user `UserConfig` (provider keys, machine fleet, active machine, draft agent). Never sent to the browser.
- **Tools**: 23 built-in (terminal, filesystem, browser, vision, code, memory, schedule, search, image, audio, delegate) + 17 service routes across MCP, CLI, plugin skills, personal skills, and optional Cursor SDK delegation.
- **Skills**: 95 SKILL.md files auto-loaded by intent matching.
- **MCP integrations**: Vercel, Stripe, Supabase, Linear, GitHub, Slack, PostHog, Sentry, Clerk, Firebase, Figma, Shopify, ClickHouse, Datadog, AWS, Cloudflare, Anthropic, OpenAI.
- **Inference**: Dedalus is the default OpenAI-compatible endpoint. The CLI can point `DEDALUS_CHAT_BASE_URL` at another compatible `/v1` endpoint.

## Important pages

- [Landing](https://www.agent-machines.dev/) -- product overview, hero, capabilities, runtime visualization grid, loadout preview, components showcase, skills manifest, architecture flow.
- [FAQ](https://www.agent-machines.dev/faq) -- current product answers, including provider status, path boundaries, tools, and machine data.
- [Terms](https://www.agent-machines.dev/terms) -- terms of service.
- [Privacy](https://www.agent-machines.dev/privacy) -- privacy policy and data boundary notes.
- [Sign in](https://www.agent-machines.dev/sign-in) -- Clerk-managed allowlist; sign in to provision a machine.
- [Onboarding](https://www.agent-machines.dev/onboarding) -- 5-step wizard (agent / skills / tools / key / boot) for first-time users.
- [GitHub](https://github.com/Kevin-Liu-01/agent-machines) -- source code, MIT license.
- [Hermes](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent) -- the Nous Research Hermes agent.
- [OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw) -- the OpenClaw computer-use agent.
- [Dedalus Machines](https://docs.dedaluslabs.ai/dcs) -- the default microVM provider.
- [Cursor SDK](https://cursor.com/docs/sdk/typescript) -- the codework delegation surface.

## Optional

- Reticle / Sigil design system: structural-visibility aesthetic with hairline rules, margin hatching, cross marks at rail intersections, all rendered in Nacelle (sans) + Geist Mono (mono) + Instrument Serif italic (wordmark only).
- The agent's ranking of which interface to use per service is documented in `tool-hierarchy.mdc` (mirrored on the public Loadout page).
